title: The N Word

jokes.text: Larry, you're disgusting.
jokes.setup: Larry does a nice gesture for Auntie Rae, clearing a garden area, creating warm moment
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae's sudden disgust reaction and running away
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds up sweet moment then crashes it. Classic Curb structure with Larry's good intentions backfiring spectacularly.

jokes.text: You know, she went over the appropriate amount of time that I can have human contact without getting aroused. I only have five seconds.
jokes.setup: Larry needs to explain why Auntie Rae called him disgusting
jokes.punchline: His clinical, matter-of-fact explanation of his five-second arousal rule
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry - turning inappropriate behavior into pseudo-scientific analysis. The specificity of 'five seconds' is perfect.

jokes.text: Not me. I've got discipline down there. I have a very discerning penis.
jokes.setup: Larry admits his lack of control
jokes.punchline: Jeff's proud declaration of having a 'discerning penis'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Larry's confession. 'Discerning penis' is memorable phrasing that fits Jeff's character.

jokes.text: My penis is an animal. It's a feral tiger, yeah.
jokes.setup: Penis control discussion continues
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly dramatic metaphors for his lack of self-control
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but feels like expected Larry hyperbole rather than fresh insight.

jokes.text: My dog howls from my snoring. I wake up my dog.
jokes.setup: Jeff explains he's getting surgery for snoring
jokes.punchline: His snoring is so bad it wakes up and disturbs his dog
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about snoring severity. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Bald people get discriminated against constantly. And you just saw a perfect example of it.
jokes.setup: Waitress was extra nice to Jeff but not Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately attributes it to bald discrimination
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry paranoia and persecution complex. Sets up ongoing theme of bald discrimination.

jokes.text: You know, if I were bald-- if I woke up tomorrow and I found myself bald, I'd be so cool with it. It'd be no big deal.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismisses Larry's bald discrimination claims
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confident assertion he'd handle baldness well (dramatic irony for later)
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for later payoff when Jeff goes bald. The dramatic irony makes this very rewatchable.

jokes.text: I could have sworn when I left, I had about twice as many french fries as there are now.
jokes.setup: Larry returns from pharmacy
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive way of accusing Jeff of eating his fries
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry petty grievance delivered in his characteristic indirect style. Good character moment.

jokes.text: No idea.
jokes.setup: Larry's obvious accusation about the french fries
jokes.punchline: Jeff's completely unconvincing denial
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan delivery. The repetition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't want to impinge on a friendship.
jokes.setup: Larry asks doctor to witness Jeff eating his fries
jokes.punchline: Doctor's overly formal word choice for this petty situation
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between formal medical language and trivial french fry dispute.

jokes.text: Impinge away. Go ahead and impinge.
jokes.setup: Doctor uses word 'impinge'
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately adopts and repeats the fancy word
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the doctor's word choice. Larry's enthusiasm for the formal language is funny.

jokes.text: I took one. Shoot me. One. Two of them might have been stuck together.
jokes.setup: Jeff finally admits to taking fries
jokes.punchline: His immediate backtracking and excuse-making
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from denial to confession to excuse. Realistic friend behavior.

jokes.text: God damn, Larry. What the fuck, man? You hug my auntie, man, you stab her in the stomach?
jokes.setup: Leon confronts Larry about the earlier incident with Auntie Rae
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude but accurate description of what happened
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Leon's blunt, colorful language. 'Stab her in the stomach' is a vivid euphemism.

jokes.text: He got a five-second rule. One, two, three, four, five-- boing, it come out.
jokes.setup: Leon explains Larry's arousal timeline
jokes.punchline: Leon's sound effect and physical description
jokes.explanation: Leon's interpretation of Larry's rule with sound effects makes it funnier. Good callback execution.

jokes.text: Where's your 'mississippi'? You gotta put mississippi in. That's how you count-- 'one-mississippi, two-mississippi, three-mississippi, four-mississippi, five-mississippi.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's five-second rule
jokes.punchline: Loretta's insistence on proper counting methodology
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation - taking Larry's arbitrary rule and demanding technical precision. Loretta's practical mindset shines.

jokes.text: That's way too long. Way too long. You're stretching it out for--
jokes.setup: Loretta demonstrates proper mississippi counting
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff immediately rejecting the longer timeframe
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the counting debate. Shows Larry's self-serving interpretation of his own rule.

jokes.text: Oh, Dr. Flomm. This is Larry David calling. Good, I met you earlier in the cafeteria. Would you like to make an appointment? Uh, no, actually. I'm calling for, um, a date.
jokes.setup: Larry calls the doctor he met
jokes.punchline: His awkward transition from medical appointment to date request
jokes.explanation: Mildly awkward but not exceptional. The misdirection works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh jeez, I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Doctor says she's with a patient
jokes.punchline: Larry's mortified realization he interrupted medical work for a date
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward moment. Larry's embarrassment is relatable but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I'm with a patient right now. Oh jeez, I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for a date during work hours
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of Larry not realizing she's with a patient
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward Larry moment

jokes.text: And I recommend being five minutes early.
jokes.setup: Making date plans with Larry
jokes.punchline: Doctor treating the date like a medical appointment
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - doctor can't turn off professional mode even for dating. Efficient and character-specific.

jokes.text: Well, that makes it easy.
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward explanation about the Black family
jokes.punchline: Sheila's dry response to the racial name coincidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - four words capture the awkwardness of Larry's observation

jokes.text: You know, I've got people in my house too. You're kidding. No. You know, it's great and then sometimes it's not so great. Well, I took in a homeless family... from the hurricane. Their name is 'black.' and they're actually black. Well, that makes it easy. Exactly, yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry and Sheila bond over having houseguests
jokes.punchline: The coincidence that the Black family is actually black, making it 'easy'
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay setup for later misunderstandings. The coincidence is mildly amusing but will become important.

jokes.text: Watch out for my testicles right over there.
jokes.setup: Doctor is demonstrating groin massage technique
jokes.punchline: Larry's unnecessary and awkward warning
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overshare. Makes an already awkward medical situation more uncomfortable unnecessarily.

jokes.text: Why don't we move this to my bedroom?
jokes.setup: Medical groin examination in living room
jokes.punchline: Doctor's innocent medical suggestion sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre. Decent misdirection but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: We talked for 45 minutes about my groin injury.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Jeff about his great date
jokes.punchline: What he considers romantic conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's skewed perspective on romance. His genuine enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I think it might make you feel better.
jokes.setup: Sheila gives Larry a note before he goes to bathroom
jokes.punchline: The mysterious note that will cause major problems
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later payoff. The innocent gesture will spiral into disaster - classic Curb structure.

jokes.text: Yeah, I fucked my wrist or something. Yeah, I was moving a futon. And I had to take the top because of this 300-lb nigger.
jokes.setup: Larry overhears phone conversation in bathroom
jokes.punchline: The racial slur that will cause massive problems
jokes.explanation: Pivotal moment that drives the entire episode. The casual racism Larry overhears becomes the source of all chaos. Well-structured setup.

jokes.text: And he's upset and he's complaining, you know, he had to move some furniture. And he says to his friend, 'I hurt my hand because I had to take the top because of this 300-lb nigger who was--'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain what he overheard to demonstrate the man's awfulness
jokes.punchline: Larry quotes the slur, making himself look like the racist
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation. Larry's good intentions backfire spectacularly when context is lost. Perfect Curb Your Enthusiasm logic.

jokes.text: What did you say? What did you just say? Oh... What did you just fix your mouth to say?
jokes.setup: Larry quotes the racist he overheard
jokes.punchline: Woman's immediate outraged reaction
jokes.explanation: Strong reaction beat. Her escalating outrage and 'fix your mouth to say' phrasing is authentic and powerful.

jokes.text: You bald son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Woman's fury at Larry continues
jokes.punchline: She adds 'bald' as an extra insult
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier bald discrimination discussion. Even in her rage, she includes his baldness as an insult.

jokes.text: Bald?
jokes.setup: Woman storms off after calling him names
jokes.punchline: Of all the insults, Larry focuses on 'bald'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment. After being accused of racism, his priority is the bald comment. Shows his warped perspective brilliantly.

jokes.text: Oh my god! No no! What? Oh my god! Look at me!
jokes.setup: Jeff wakes up from surgery
jokes.punchline: His horror at discovering his head was shaved
jokes.explanation: Massive payoff to Jeff's earlier confidence. His immediate panic contradicts everything he said about being cool with baldness.

jokes.text: I was coming to see you in the operating room and I had just been victimized by a terrible incident in the cafeteria.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains why he shaved Jeff's head
jokes.punchline: The cafeteria 'incident' caused him to make surgical errors
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Larry's actions ripple outward. The doctor's emotional response affecting his medical judgment.

jokes.text: He was just a despicable bald man.
jokes.setup: Doctor describes the racist he encountered
jokes.punchline: His description perfectly matches Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the revelation. The doctor unknowingly describes Larry to Larry, building tension for the realization.

jokes.text: Look at him! Look at him! What the fuck? I'm bald! You like this look? He had a beautiful full head of hair... a mane that I loved and now he looks like you!
jokes.setup: Susie sees Jeff's shaved head
jokes.punchline: Her brutal comparison of Jeff to Larry
jokes.explanation: Susie's raw fury is hilarious. 'Now he looks like you!' is a devastating insult that perfectly captures her horror.

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with being bald! Sure there is! Yes, there is, Larry!
jokes.setup: Larry defends baldness
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier themes. Everyone's immediate rejection of Larry's position is funny and realistic.

jokes.text: You said you wouldn't care if you were bald! Yeah, in 40 years! Not fucking today!
jokes.setup: Larry reminds Jeff of his earlier confidence
jokes.punchline: Jeff's panicked clarification about timeline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and escalation. Jeff's desperate distinction between gradual vs sudden baldness is perfectly human and hilarious.

jokes.text: Yours is gonna grow back. Mine isn't! That's right. You know what? Curse on you! Mine's gonna grow back. Yours isn't! You deserve it!
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes Larry caused this
jokes.punchline: Larry's cruel celebration of his advantage
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny. Larry's vindictive glee at his friend's misfortune shows his worst qualities in an amusing way.

jokes.text: Did I just hear the word nigger come out your mouth? No, but I was-- Did you say nigger? No no! Leon! Leon! What? Oh no no, don't-- Leon, get your ass out here. Larry just said nigger.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain the cafeteria incident to his houseguests
jokes.punchline: The same misunderstanding happens again, but worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation. Larry repeats his exact mistake with worse consequences. The structure of escalating misunderstandings is brilliant.

jokes.text: Get thfuck out of here! Larry just said nigger! How you gonna say nigger, man? Is that how you feel about us? That we a bunch of niggers up in here?
jokes.setup: Leon hears Larry allegedly used racial slur
jokes.punchline: Leon's escalating outrage and accusations
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation showing the real impact of the misunderstanding. Leon's genuine hurt and anger adds weight to the comedy.

jokes.text: First, you're gonna be rubbing all up against me, then whacking off in the damn car! Now you call us-- I was massaging!
jokes.setup: Auntie Rae lists Larry's offenses
jokes.punchline: Her interpretation of the groin massage as masturbation
jokes.explanation: Great callback bringing together multiple misunderstandings. Auntie Rae's moral outrage escalating through the episode.

jokes.text: Fuck you, Larry, wit' your monkey ass!
jokes.setup: Leon and family are leaving Larry's house
jokes.punchline: Leon's parting insult using animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Leon's creative insult fits his personality and adds to the escalating departure.

jokes.text: I didn't just fall off the melon truck.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching to Ben Stiller about his experience
jokes.punchline: Larry's mangled version of 'didn't fall off the turnip truck'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry malapropism. His confident delivery of the wrong phrase is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Stiller said no. What? Doesn't want to work with me. Are you kidding? Doesn't feel it's right.
jokes.setup: Larry's confident pitch to Ben Stiller
jokes.punchline: Stiller's rejection without explanation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's earlier overconfidence. The vague rejection adds to the mystery and comedy.

jokes.text: You know what doesn't feel right about it? You're bald. Doesn't want a bald man to be representing him!
jokes.setup: Trying to understand Stiller's rejection
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately blames bald discrimination
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency. Larry's one-track mind always returns to bald discrimination as the explanation for everything.

jokes.text: That's the way it works, my friend. Welcome to my world.
jokes.setup: Jeff experiencing bald discrimination for first time
jokes.punchline: Larry's smug welcome to the bald community
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's vindictive satisfaction at Jeff joining his plight. The smugness is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: There's, like, meetings? There's no meetings, but we see each other on the street, we nod. We'll give a thumbs up. You know what? We love each other, me and my bald brothers.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks about bald support system
jokes.punchline: Larry's description of informal bald brotherhood
jokes.explanation: Good world-building around Larry's bald community. The casual solidarity and 'bald brothers' phrase is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Larry, had a great time last night. Would love to get together friday night at the bel-air regency hotel... just the two of us. So tired of all these brothers and sisters around. I know you feel the same way. Tell me your life wouldn't be better without the blacks.
jokes.setup: Pharmacist finally deciphers Sheila's note
jokes.punchline: The note's innocent meaning sounds horribly racist when read aloud
jokes.explanation: Masterful payoff to the handwriting plot. The innocent note sounds horrifically racist due to context about the Black family. Brilliant misdirection.

jokes.text: Oh, okay, uh... No no no no no. It's your business. Here's your prescription. No, see I've got-- Take your prescription.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain the misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: The pharmacist wanting nothing to do with Larry's perceived racism
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the misunderstanding - Larry can't explain without making it worse

jokes.text: And as far as what happened earlier, I'm mean, you know, usually like, five-mississippi. Honest to god, five-mississippi-- That's all I need. Why don't you just go?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain himself to Sheila after the note misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: He brings up the arousal timing issue at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry move - bringing up inappropriate topic at worst possible time. The callback to the timing discussion is well-executed.

jokes.text: Somebody go in there and put a sock in that motherfucker's mouth!
jokes.setup: Jeff's terrible snoring is keeping everyone awake
jokes.punchline: Leon's colorful complaint about Jeff's snoring
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jeff's snoring issue. Leon's colorful language and the family returning adds nice circularity to episode.

jokes.text: Jeff's snore? Man, I'm telling you, that was awful. Worse than the damn hurricane.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining why the family returned
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jeff's snoring to a natural disaster
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - comparing snoring to Hurricane Katrina is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Talk to your boy, man. Shit, that motherfucker swallowed a kazoo.
jokes.setup: Jeff's horrific snoring continues
jokes.punchline: Leon's creative metaphor for the snoring sound
jokes.explanation: Great creative metaphor from Leon. 'Swallowed a kazoo' perfectly captures both the sound and absurdity of severe snoring.

jokes.text: One-mississippi, two-mississippi, three-mississippi... My own daughter locks herself in her room, won't come out whenever I'm home.
jokes.setup: Larry hugs Jeff while he's vulnerable about baldness impact
jokes.punchline: Larry counts mississippi while Jeff shares painful personal details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback execution. Larry applying his arousal timing rule during Jeff's emotional moment is perfectly inappropriate and character-consistent.

jokes.text: You know, it's-- It's a thing we all do. Don't have to be embarrassed. No need for embarrassment.
jokes.setup: Larry testifies about going to bathroom at hearing
jokes.punchline: His unnecessary elaboration on universal bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-explanation. His tendency to belabor obvious points in formal settings is character-appropriate but mild.

jokes.text: Generally, when I use a public toilet, I'll go into the stall as opposed to the urinals. I find there's, you know, there's no privacy.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his bathroom habits to the medical board
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily detailed explanation of toilet preferences
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-sharing - turning simple testimony into uncomfortable detail

jokes.text: Generally, when I-- When I use a public toilet, I'll-- I'll go into the stall as opposed to the urinals. I find there's, you know, there's no privacy. And if there's no dividers, it's kind of like going in a trough.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his bathroom habits to medical board
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed analysis of urinal vs. stall preferences
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry rambling. The 'trough' comparison is mildly funny but the whole bit goes on too long without strong payoff.

jokes.text: Mr. David, tell us what the man said! Just say it! You are wasting this committee's time. Larry, tell 'em. Please tell us what the man said. Say it! Tell 'em! Will you tell them? Just say it! Larry, just tell 'em! Tell us what the man said! Tell them, please? Just do it, Larry! Tell us now, Mr. David! Just say it! Larry, tell 'em!
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to repeat the racial slur at the hearing
jokes.punchline: Everyone pressuring him to say the very word that caused all the problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic escalation. The irony that everyone demands he say the word that got him in trouble is brilliantly constructed. Great ending tension.

tmdb_overview: Larry accidentally offends Lorreta's Auntie Rae and takes advantage of Jeff's hospitalization.
